summary: postinstall pipes unpinned remote shell script to sh
details: scripts/postinstall.js executes at npm install time and runs `npm install -g @opencode/cli 2>/dev/null || curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install.sh 2>/dev/null | sh`. The fallback fetches an unpinned, mutable shell script from opencode.ai (a third-party domain that does not match the package publisher) and pipes it directly to `sh` with no hash or signature verification. The primary branch performs a privileged global npm install silently. On any `npm install` of this package, the installer's machine executes whatever bytes opencode.ai serves at that moment, with no integrity check and no opt-out. This is the canonical curl|sh dropper shape — even if today's content is benign, the installer is permanently subject to whatever that URL serves in the future.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
